# Data Preparation and Feature Engineering {#ch-preparation}

::: epigraph
Trust, but verify.

*Ronald Reagan*
:::

<!-- DEVELOPMENT OUTLINE: preserve this marker until the prose is drafted. -->

## Data Quality and Provenance

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-data-quality-and-provenance)
to turn **Data Quality and Provenance** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Missing, Noisy, and Inconsistent Data

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-missing-noisy-and-inconsistent-data)
to turn **Missing, Noisy, and Inconsistent Data** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Scaling, Normalization, and Transformation

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-scaling-normalization-and-transformation)
to turn **Scaling, Normalization, and Transformation** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Encoding Categorical and Structured Variables

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-encoding-categorical-and-structured-variables)
to turn **Encoding Categorical and Structured Variables** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Feature Selection and Dimensionality Reduction

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-feature-selection-and-dimensionality-reduction)
to turn **Feature Selection and Dimensionality Reduction** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Leakage and Reproducible Pipelines

<!-- practice-link:start -->
::: {.callout-tip collapse="true" title="Put it into practice"}
Continue with [the corresponding practice activity](../practice/06-data-preparation-and-feature-engineering.qmd#sec-pr-06-leakage-and-reproducible-pipelines)
to turn **Leakage and Reproducible Pipelines** into a calculation,
implementation, visualization, diagnostic, or interpretation task.
:::
<!-- practice-link:end -->

## Summary and Exercises

There is no perfect data. In fact, a Kaggle 2017 survey showed that
*dirty data* \"is the most common problem for workers in the data
science realm.\"[@Kaggle:2017] About 50% of survey responses cited it as
a barrier at work.
